cards.title: TRACES
cards.note: An empirical reserve of datasets with natural out-of-distribution shift, structured for mechanism-discovery benchmarking. Private — witness exposure to decide.
cards.author: Dhruv Gupta

cards.title: Benchmark Curation · Conformance as Typechecking
cards.direction: Corpus admission is stated as typechecking: a dataset manifest is well-formed only when a conjunction of machine-checked propositions holds over it. Constraints that could be enforced at the type level were, which eliminated two classes of ill-formed manifest states; the remaining content-bearing rules, natural-shift origin, regime-schema consistency, and corpus-level referential integrity, are stated as propositions over the manifest and the corpus.

witnesses.claim: Admission rules for a dataset manifest, machine-checked as a single conjunction of propositions.
Regime-schema consistency stated as a content-bearing decidable proposition.
Corpus-level referential integrity stated as a universally quantified proposition.
The natural-shift admission criterion, stated as a decidable proposition.
witnesses.code:
def A1_NaturalShift (m : DatasetManifest) : Prop :=
  m.dataOrigin.primary ≠ DataOriginPrimary.synthetic

witnesses.claim: The dependent-type encoding eliminated two classes of ill-formed manifest states, leaving this axiom tautological.

cards.author: Dhruv Gupta

cards.title: Distribution Shift · Environment Pairs
cards.direction: The primitive experimental unit for out-of-distribution evaluation is a directed pair: a training environment and a distinct test environment under shift. Structural constraints on pairs, such as the number of member datasets each pair type admits, are carried as proofs in the data structure.

witnesses.claim: The environment pair as the primitive experimental unit for out-of-distribution evaluation.
witnesses.code:
def A2_PairPrimitive (p : DatasetPair) : Prop :=
  p.trainEnv ≠ [] ∧ p.oodTestEnv ≠ [] ∧ p.trainEnv ≠ p.oodTestEnv

witnesses.claim: Member arity for each pair type is enforced as a proof carried in the data structure.

cards.author: Dhruv Gupta

cards.title: Provenance · Typed Records
cards.direction: File-level and epistemic provenance are both typed. A checksum is a refinement subtype whose SHA-256 digest format is verified when the value is constructed, and the epistemic state recorded for each dataset holds facts, hypotheses, intuitions, and unknowns in a single level-indexed structure, extending datasheet-style dataset documentation to epistemics.
cards.note: Discharging the digest check with native_decide rather than kernel reduction widens the trusted base; the development documents this trust-boundary extension explicitly.

witnesses.claim: Digest well-formedness is enforced at construction through a SHA-256 refinement subtype.
A level-indexed record with a dependent boundary field replaces three duplicate structures.
witnesses.code:
structure EpistemicState (level : EpistemicLevel) where
  facts hypotheses intuitions unknowns : List String := []
  boundary : level.BoundaryType

cards.author: Dhruv Gupta
